mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC signaling to reduce AML cell proliferation (notably in FLT3-mutant contexts)
additional_notes: Alternative name in text: PKC/FLT3 inhibitor; noted as approved for FLT3+ AML

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL kinases to block oncogenic signaling in AML
Selective inhibition of FLT3 signaling to suppress proliferation in FLT3-driven AML
additional_notes: Noted as approved in some regions

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 and RAF to reduce proliferation and signaling
additional_notes: Mentions serum binding can shift apparent potency

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family kinases and ABL to disrupt signaling pathways relevant in AML subsets
additional_notes: Text notes activity in subsets and pathway crosstalk in AML

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to suppress cytokine-driven JAK-STAT signaling
additional_notes: Context: cytokine/JAK-STAT dependence in some AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to increase histone acetylation and alter gene expression
additional_notes: Noted as approved in myeloma; AML activity in vitro

mechanism: HDAC inhibition leading to transcriptional changes and growth arrest/apoptosis
Weak HDAC inhibition leading to epigenetic modulation and differentiation effects
additional_notes: Clinically used; described as weaker potency

mechanism: Nucleoside analog that inhibits DNA methyltransferases, causing DNA hypomethylation
additional_notes: Approved in MDS/AML; note: often needs longer exposure (96–120 h) per text

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA/DNA and inhibits DNA methylation, promoting hypomethylation
additional_notes: Approved; note: often longer exposure per text

mechanism: Inhibits XPO1/CRM1-mediated nuclear export, retaining tumor suppressors in the nucleus
additional_notes: Noted as approved in myeloma; AML activity reported

mechanism: Selective BCL-2 inhibition to promote mitochondrial apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; serum binding noted as affecting potency

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-XL to trigger apoptosis (less selective than venetoclax)
additional_notes: Alternative name in text: ABT-263; thrombocytopenia in vivo but useful in vitro; serum binding noted

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in myeloma; AML in vitro activity noted

mechanism: Irreversible proteasome inhibition leading to accumulation of ubiquitinated proteins and apoptosis

category: Metabolism/stress-response repurposing candidate (mitochondrial complex I modulator)
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMP-associated stress pathways

category: Metabolism/stress-response repurposing candidate (mitochondrial electron transport inhibitor)
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial electron transport (commonly via complex III/cytochrome bc1), reducing oxidative phosphorylation
additional_notes: Described as antimalarial with anticancer reports

mechanism: Modulates ALDH activity and oxidative stress; cytotoxicity often enhanced with copper
additional_notes: Optionally test with copper (± 1–5 µM CuCl2); copper-dependent cytotoxicity noted

category: Metabolism/stress-response repurposing candidate (antimalarial; ROS/ferroptosis-like stress inducer)
mechanism: Induces oxidative stress (ROS) and ferroptosis-like cell death pathways in some cancer contexts
additional_notes: Described as antimalarial; ROS/ferroptosis-like stress mentioned
